pairs.question: What was the Convention of 1836 and when did it take place?
pairs.answer: The Convention of 1836 was a meeting of elected delegates held in Washington-on-the-Brazos, Texas, in March 1836. It occurred during the Texas Revolution and resulted in the declaration of Texas independence from Mexico.

pairs.question: Who was the primary author of the Texas Declaration of Independence?
pairs.answer: The Texas Declaration of Independence was written by George Childress.

pairs.question: What was the interim government of Texas called before the Convention of 1836?
pairs.answer: The interim government of Texas before the Convention of 1836 was known as the Consultation.

pairs.question: Why did delegates at the Convention of 1836 choose to focus on drafting a constitution despite news of the Alamo siege?
pairs.answer: Delegate and commander-in-chief Sam Houston convinced the delegates to continue working on the constitution rather than rush to aid the soldiers at the Alamo. He likely prioritized establishing a stable government to better organize the Texas Revolution's efforts.

pairs.question: What role did Antonio Lopez de Santa Anna play in the events leading up to the Convention of 1836?
pairs.answer: Mexican President Antonio Lopez de Santa Anna led a large army into Texas to suppress the revolt. His forces arrived near San Antonio de Bexar in late February 1836, just before the Convention convened, and later marched toward Washington-on-the-Brazos after the fall of the Alamo.

pairs.question: How did the delegates at the Convention of 1836 structure the new Texas government?
pairs.answer: Delegates elected an interim government led by President David G. Burnet and drafted a Texas Constitution primarily modeled after the Constitution of the United States.

pairs.question: What immediate consequence did the fall of the Alamo have on the new Texas government?
pairs.answer: The fall of the Alamo prompted Santa Anna's army to march toward Washington-on-the-Brazos, forcing the new Texas government to flee the location.
